name: sci-review
description: Review the research manuscript (breadth then depth) and fix findings until no Blocker or Major remains.
rubric: The breadth pass scanned the current draft for missing sections, uncited claims, and results stated without analysis.
The depth pass returned an explicit verdict for each of the five gates (Reproducibility, Citation Integrity, Statistical Validity, Claim Substantiation, Novelty & Scope).
Zero Blocker findings remain (e.g., an invented reference, a conclusion unsupported by results, a missing Methods section).
Zero Major findings remain (e.g., a bare estimate reported without uncertainty, causal language on correlational data).
The stated contribution is consistent with the results and positioned against prior work.
fixPrompt: Each iteration: (1) breadth-review the current manuscript for completeness and internal consistency; (2) depth-review by issuing a verdict on each gate in gates.md; (3) if no Blocker/Major findings remain and sci-validate is green, converge — report Minor/Nit findings without iterating; (4) otherwise fix the Blocker/Major findings in the manuscript and re-run sci-validate before the next pass.
guardrails: Findings are fixed in the manuscript, not argued away — a gate failure means the analysis changes, not the verdict text.
Do not soften a limitation or drop a citation to clear a gate.
Re-review the fixes themselves each iteration, not just the prior findings list.
onExhausted: Stop. Report the remaining Blocker/Major findings, the fix attempted for each, and recommend human reviewer review or a return to the Design stage.
